pages.title: How Trait Levels Work
pages.text: Every eligible component contributes its material's trait, regardless of whether it is a head, core, guard, binding, wrap, or handle.$(p)The first level supplies the complete identity. Repeated components add half as much each time:$(li)Level I: 100%$(li)Level II: 150%$(li)Level III: 175%$(p)Traits do not consume enchantment capacity.

pages.title: Wooden Handles
pages.text: $(bold)Steady — oak/wood/plant fiber$()$(li)×1.25 mining speed$(li)+0.35 attack speed$(li)Repairs restore 50% more$(p)$(bold)Tensioned — spider-silk string$()$(li)Crossbows gain effective Quick Charge II$(li)Projectile damage ×1.30$(p)Leather is an armour material, not an active tool-trait source.

pages.title: Elemental Handles
pages.text: $(bold)Swift — breeze$()$(li)×1.60 mining speed$(li)+0.75 attack speed$(li)×2 durability wear$(p)$(bold)Kindled — blaze$()$(li)Tool is fireproof$(li)Eligible block drops are smelted$(li)Damaged targets burn for 5 seconds

pages.title: Iron and Copper
pages.text: $(bold)Reinforced — iron$()$(li)Prevents 75% of durability wear$(li)A repair material restores 50% durability$(p)$(bold)Work-Hardened — copper$()$(li)Below 75% condition: +25% mining/damage$(li)Below 50%: +50%$(li)Below 25%: +100%

pages.title: Gold
pages.text: $(bold)Gilded — gold$()$(p)With Better Enchanting, every gold component grants $(bold)+1 enchantment capacity$().$(p)Gilded no longer grants extra experience. Gold's strength is flexibility: it supplies another enchantment choice while another material can supply a stronger fixed trait.
